channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Stakeholders Propose Wetland Conservation Measures in Tanzania
item.description: Environmental stakeholders in Tanzania have identified four key measures to address wetland degradation, including community participation, alternative livelihoods, stricter law enforcement, and inter...

item.title: Zazzau & Gombe Emirs Collaborate for National Unity and Peace
item.description: The Emir of Gombe, Abubakar Shehu Abubakar III, met with the Emir of Zazzau, Mallam Ahmed Nuhu Bamalli, to discuss strengthening collaboration between their emirates. Both leaders emphasized the impor...

item.title: Security forces kill 19 militants as 11 soldiers embrace martyrdom during intelligence-based operation in Balochistan
item.description: At least 19 militants were killed while 11 security personnel embraced martyrdom during an intelligence-based operation conducted along the N-25 highway in Balochistan, according to security sources. ...

item.title: Six Demonstrators Freed on Bail After Prosecution Drops Charges in Saba Saba Protests
item.description: Six demonstrators accused of blocking traffic during the July 7 Saba Saba protests have been released on bond after the prosecution declined to oppose their bail application. The case, heard at Milima...

item.title: Trump's Activities During the World Cup and Corruption Allegations
item.description: The article, part of Crikey's 'Drowned Out' series, examines Donald Trump's political maneuvers during the FIFA World Cup, highlighting his controversial actions and the broader context of U.S. politi...

item.title: India's fragmented approach to pesticide regulation overlooks cumulative exposure risks
item.description: The article highlights how India's current pesticide regulation system treats food, water, and air exposure pathways as separate issues, leading to underestimation of health risks. It cites incidents ...

item.title: NACC halts Brereton inquiry, probes JFK archives, and covers political figures' online engagements
item.description: The article details the National Archives and Crime Commission (NACC) inspector's decision to halt an investigation into former commissioner Paul Brereton, citing unnecessary scrutiny of a non-current...

item.title: Tamil Nadu and Kerala's Opposition to Food Security Law Amendments Explained
item.description: The article discusses the opposition from Tamil Nadu and Kerala to proposed amendments in the National Food Security Act (NFSA). The Centre plans to shift from a household-based entitlement system to ...

item.title: Canadian Universities Launch $40M Venture Fund to Support Life Sciences Startups
item.description: University of Toronto and McMaster University have partnered with Genesys Capital to launch a $40-million venture fund aimed at supporting life sciences startups, particularly spinouts from both insti...

item.title: Iran-US tensions escalate as attacks on oil hubs disrupt regional ceasefire
item.description: The article details escalating tensions between Iran and the United States following Iran's targeted strikes on U.S. military installations in Bahrain and Kuwait. These actions come amid ongoing dispu...

item.title: Federal Judge Blocks DOJ Subpoena for 2020 Election Workers' Info
item.description: A federal judge has ruled that the U.S. Department of Justice cannot obtain the names and personal contact information of all individuals who worked during the 2020 election in Fulton County, Georgia....

item.title: U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Breaks Down Amid Escalating Military Tensions and Oil Price Surge
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump declared an interim agreement to end the war with Iran as 'over' following renewed military clashes between the two nations. The conflict escalated after Iran launched atta...

item.title: Teen Dies After Missing Following Boat Trip with Friends
item.description: Nolan Xavier Wells, an 18-year-old from Ocean Springs, Mississippi, died tragically after failing to return from a boat trip with friends to Horn Island. His mother, Christine Wonsley, reported him mi...

item.title: Marjorie Taylor Greene Criticizes Trump After Iran Ceasefire Collapses and Regional Tensions Rise
item.description: President Donald Trump announced that the three-week-old ceasefire involving Iran was effectively over after being asked about renewed attacks during a NATO summit in Turkey. Trump said he believed th...

item.title: Sokoto Council Chairman Resigns Over Administrative Challenges
item.description: Hon. Isa Kalanjeni, Executive Chairman of Tangaza Local Government Area in Sokoto State, has resigned from his post and the All Progressives Congress (APC) due to administrative challenges and persona...

item.title: Madlanga Commission Continues Witness Hearings on Justice System Allegations
item.description: The Madlanga commission of inquiry, which is investigating allegations of criminality, political interference, and corruption within the criminal justice system, is set to hear further witness testimo...

item.title: Former Justice Adolfo Azcuna Opposes Raising Conviction Threshold for Sara Duterte Impeachment Trial
item.description: Former Supreme Court associate justice Adolfo Azcuna has stated that there is no immediate need to raise the 16-vote conviction threshold required to convict Vice President Sara Duterte before the Sup...

item.title: Analysis of the 2026 Genesis Scottish Open Betting Favorites and Value Picks at The Renaissance Club
item.description: The article provides a detailed betting preview for the 2026 Genesis Scottish Open at The Renaissance Club in North Berwick, Scotland, an event co-sanctioned by the PGA TOUR and DP World Tour that ser...

item.title: Ontario Colleges Skip Efficiency Reviews Amid Financial Challenges
item.description: Six of Ontario’s publicly-funded colleges chose not to apply to a government fund designed to identify operational efficiencies, including Conestoga College, which faces severe financial turmoil. The ...

item.title: Kisumu Activists Skip Saba Saba Protests Over Security Fears
item.description: Kisumu activists have called off planned protests during the Saba Saba commemorations due to fears of violence and criminal infiltration. Human rights defenders like Willy Otiende and Brenda Beverly e...
